data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Rally as Fed Eyes Rate Hikes to Counter Inflation; Industrial Sector Posts Solid Growth
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed Wednesday in positive territory, although a warning from the Fed put investors on high alert.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Instead of rate cuts, higher interest rates could be on the table if inflation remains above 2%, minutes from January's Fed meeting showed. Inflation remains a key concern for many officials, with several considering the "possibility that upward adjustments to the target range for the federal funds rate could be appropriate if inflation remains at above-target levels."

The minutes also noted that a majority of officials believe the risk of persistent inflation is still present, while downside risks to employment have softened in recent months. Lower rates can benefit the labor market but risk pushing inflation higher through increased borrowing and consumer spending.

The Fed will announce its next interest rate decision on March 18, with 94.1% odds of rates staying unchanged on the CME FedWatch tool. By year-end, the most likely scenario is for the central bank to cut rates by 50 bps to 3.00%-3.25%.

Meanwhile, the Trump administration's goal of increasing domestic production is gaining ground. In January, U.S. industrial production rose by 0.7% from the prior month, marking the highest rate of growth since February 2025. Several factors may have contributed to the growth, including easing trade uncertainty and favorable tax policies. Efficiency is also on the rise, with factory capacity utilization reaching 75.6% during the month, the highest since September and improving from 75.2% in December.

Elsewhere, the S&P 500 (SPX) is having its worst start to the year compared to global peers since 1995. The benchmark index is essentially flat year-to-date, while the iShares MSCI ACWI ex U.S. ETF (ACWX), which tracks global markets excluding the U.S., has returned 7.4%. Geopolitical tensions and volatile economic policies, including tariffs, President Trump's threat to annex Greenland, and concerns about overspending in the AI industry, have pushed many investors to shift their capital away from the U.S.

U.S. tensions with Iran have also unsettled some investors. While both sides touted progress in nuclear talks on Tuesday, Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei continues to send out aggressive statements on X, including a post that warned the U.S. military "may at times be struck so hard that it cannot get up again."

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.56% gain, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) returned 0.80%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Spotify Stock (SPOT) Crashes After Google Unveils New AI Music Feature
data.description:
Shares of music streaming company Spotify (SPOT) fell 5% immediately after Google parent company Alphabet (GOOGL) unveiled a new artificial intelligence (AI) music feature.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Specifically, Alphabet announced that its Gemini AI model can now generate music and lyrics for users, raising questions about potential impacts for Spotify, which, until now, has had a strong hold on music streaming around the world.

Alphabet's new Lyria 3 music generation model, released in beta to Gemini app users, allows people to create 30-second tracks using text prompts and images. The feature can also generate vocal and instrumental compositions across various music genres.

Music for the Masses

Users of Gemini can also create music by describing their ideas in text or by uploading photos that the system analyzes to generate matching tracks. Each creation includes custom cover art made by Google's Nano Banana image generation system.

Alphabet said that it is also integrating Lyria 3 into its YouTube's Dream Track feature for content creators. YouTube, also owned by Alphabet, is another popular place for users to access music content. Analysts say the immediate reaction to SPOT stock reflects investor worries about AI's impacts on established music platforms and streaming services.

Is GOOGL Stock a Buy?

The stock of Alphabet has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 32 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 25 Buy and seven Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average GOOGL price target of $379.47 implies 25.01% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Has the Market Misjudged Palantir? Mizuho Upgrades PLTR Stock
data.description:
For much of the past year, investors have questioned whether Palantir (PLTR) is a real AI business or simply a hype-driven trade. Year-to-date, the stock has declined 24%. After the stock pulled back at the start of 2026, Mizuho analyst Gregg Moskowitz said the drop has actually created an opportunity. The analyst upgraded the shares to Outperform from Neutral and kept a $195 price target, implying about 47% upside from current levels.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The main worry was not growth but valuation. The stock surged during the AI rally and investors feared a sharp re-rating. Moskowitz said that reset has already happened. Palantir's valuation multiple fell about 46% in the first six weeks of the year even as the business improved, making the risk-reward "newly attractive."

What Is the Real Catalyst for the Upgrade?

The biggest driver behind the upgrade is Palantir's commercial business. U.S. commercial revenue grew 137% year-over-year in the last quarter, suggesting companies are moving beyond AI experiments and into real deployment.

The analyst believes enterprises are embedding Palantir's AIP software directly into daily operations, which makes the platform harder to replace and supports long-term growth.

The government segment also remains strong. U.S. government revenue grew 66% in the latest quarter and represents about 41% of total revenue. With ongoing defense modernization and geopolitical tensions, Moskowitz expects public-sector demand to stay durable.

Why the Analyst Thinks Palantir Is Different

Some investors worry Palantir does not build its own AI models, but Mizuho said that misses the point. The company's value lies in integrating data, security, and workflows so organizations can deploy AI safely inside real systems rather than simply accessing a model.

As AI models become more widely available, the analyst believes that operational layer could become even more important.

Is PLTR Stock a Buy? 

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on PLTR stock based on 12 Buys, five Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average PLTR price target of $191.25 per share implies 43.78% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Hold Steady as Investors Await Walmart Earnings
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were little changed on Wednesday evening after the major averages logged a solid session. Traders also looked ahead to Walmart's (WMT) quarterly results, due Thursday morning. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were up 0.06%, 0.01%, and 0.04%, respectively, at 6:49 p.m. EST on February 18.

During inflation-industrial-sector-posts-solid-growth">the regular session, the S&P 500, the Nasdaq, and the Dow Jones climbed 0.6%, 0.8%, and 0.3%, respectively. Tech leaders in the "Magnificent Seven," along with financial and energy stocks, helped lift the market.

Walmart's earnings on Thursday will be closely watched, given the retailer's strong performance in 2026 so far and its recent move into the $1 trillion market cap club. Also, traders are awaiting weekly jobless claims and pending home sales data due tomorrow.

data.title: Bank of America Stock (BAC) Offers Mean-Reversion Options Trade
data.description:
Bank of America (BAC) on a surface level does not represent an enticing opportunity, let's just be real about that. Since the start of the year, BAC stock has been down more than 4%, which, by most other standards, wouldn't necessarily raise an alarm. However, BAC ranks among the elite big banks. It's not supposed to be exciting. So, if we're talking about it — and not in pleasant terms — something has likely gone wrong (and it's not the CEO Brian Moynihan's new pay package).

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

A statistical phenomenon has also failed to flatter Bank of America. In January, BAC stock lost more than 3% of its equity value. So far in February, it's on pace to lose over 1%. If this month also falls into red ink, it would only be the third time since 2010. And because 2020 was such an aberrant year, I would only really consider this double-red incident to have occurred twice.
data.author: Joshua Enomoto

data.title: Fed Warns of Rate Hikes if Inflation Remains Above 2%, Minutes Show
data.description:
The minutes from January's Fed meeting showed that some Fed officials are considering hiking interest rates in order to tame inflation, which remains above the central bank's target of 2%. During the meeting, the Fed voted 10-2 to leave rates unchanged.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Several participants considered the "possibility that upward adjustments to the target range for the federal funds rate could be appropriate if inflation remains at above-target levels."

Fed Signals Patience on Rate Policy

The majority of officials agreed that persistent inflation is still a key risk, while downside risks to employment have eased in recent months. Lower interest rates can help support the labor market, although they can also increase inflation by encouraging spending and borrowing.

Some officials worried that cutting rates while inflation remains elevated could be viewed as a weakening commitment to the 2% inflation target, possibly making higher prices "more entrenched."

The next interest rate meeting is scheduled for March 18, where the Fed is widely expected to hold rates steady.

data.title: Robinhood Reveals the Most Actively Traded Assets Among Retail Investors
data.description:
Silver (SLV) and gold (GLD) have recently become some of the most actively traded assets among Robinhood (HOOD) users. Over the past two weeks, retail investors have increasingly turned to these metals as they look for ways to benefit from the growing buildout of artificial intelligence infrastructure. According to Steve Quirk, Robinhood's chief brokerage officer, interest is also rising in the energy sector through the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLE) and in infrastructure-focused funds like the iShares U.S. Infrastructure ETF (IFRA).

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the same time, Quirk told CNBC that many retail investors are shifting away from broad market ETFs and becoming more selective. A "rolling AI scare" has pressured parts of the tech sector, which has pushed traders to rethink their approach. As a result, attention has moved beyond software stocks and toward the raw materials and infrastructure that support data centers, chips, and energy demand. Precious metals, in particular, stand out because they are essential components in the AI supply chain.

Importantly, Quirk explained that physical assets feel safer during periods of market volatility and sector rotation because there is comfort in owning something tangible that is likely to benefit no matter which AI company ends up leading the race. Even so, Nvidia (NVDA) and Tesla (TSLA) remain the largest individual holdings among Robinhood customers, although many traders have been trimming those positions and rebalancing as markets fluctuate.

Which Retail Investor Favorite Is the Better Stock?

Turning to Wall Street, between the two largest individual holdings among Robinhood customers mentioned above, analysts think that NVDA stock has more room to run than TSLA. In fact, NVDA's price target of $187.88 per share implies 38.6% upside versus TSLA's 3.5% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: HLTH, ESIH, HLT: Give Your Portfolio a Lift with European Healthcare ETFs
data.description:
Investors worried about volatility in the U.S. stock market should look to Europe and several advantageous healthcare exchange-traded funds.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The American market remains volatile as investors worry about the impacts of artificial intelligence (AI), as well as the direction of interest rates and health of the economy. Market gyrations have grown more extreme since the start of 2026.

Many analysts are recommending that investors look to Europe for diversification and gains. European stocks have outperformed U.S. equities since Donald Trump returned to the White House. And Europe is home to leading healthcare companies such as Novo Nordisk (NVO), AstraZeneca (AZN), and Sanofi (SNY).

European Healthcare ETFs

Leading European healthcare ETFs include the SPDR MSCI Europe Health Care ETF (HLTH), which tracks the performance of the MSCI Europe Health Care Index, providing targeted exposure to large and mid-cap healthcare companies across developed European markets.

There's also the iShares MSCI Europe Health Care Sector ETF (ESIH), the largest and most liquid ETF that tracks the MSCI Europe Health Care index. And the Amundi STOXX Europe 600 Healthcare ETF (AU:HLTH), which follows the broader STOXX Europe 600 Healthcare index and includes 55 leading healthcare firms. Below is a chart comparing these ETFs.

data.title: CoreWeave Stock Forecast Ahead of Earnings – Here's What to Expect
data.description:
CoreWeave (CRWV) will release its fourth-quarter fiscal 2025 results after markets close on February 26. The cloud infrastructure provider has seen sharp volatility amid recent AI fears, with shares down 4.4% over the past month. CoreWeave specializes in high-performance Nvidia (NVDA) GPUs (graphics processing units) for training large AI models and serves large clients like OpenAI and Meta (META) across 33+ data centers.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Execution risk remains the prime concern for the neo-cloud provider. Investors will scrutinize management comments for signs of further delays in AI infrastructure buildouts. In Q3, summer weather halted progress at its Denton, Texas data center, forcing cuts to 2025 capex and revenue forecasts.

Expectations from CoreWeave

The Street expects CoreWeave to post an adjusted loss of $0.50 per share, significantly wider than $0.08 per share reported last year. Sales are forecast to surge about 105% year-over-year to $1.53 billion. However, compared to Q3FY25, the figure shows a modest rise of 12.5%.

Meanwhile, the company has guided for full-year 2025 sales of $5.05-5.15 billion and adjusted EBIT (earnings before interest and taxes) between $690 and $720 million.

Analysts' Views Ahead of Results

Citizens JMP analyst Gregory Miller reiterated his Buy rating and $180 price target, implying 97.8% upside potential. He stated that CoreWeave leads in renting out powerful GPUs as a service (GPUaaS) and is well-placed to meet booming AI infrastructure needs, with multi-year deals and over $55.6 billion in revenue backlog. The market for GPU rentals is exploding due to faster Gen AI adoption and big cloud companies outsourcing more.

Risks like falling prices, customer concentration, and high debt persist. Still, CoreWeave's top market position, clear future contracts, and growth potential make CRWV a strong short- to medium-term AI infrastructure play.

Similarly, D.A. Davidson analyst Alex Platt kept his Buy rating and $110 price target, suggesting 20.9% upside, after Nvidia infused another $2 billion in CoreWeave. Nvidia is helping CoreWeave reduce risks in building 5 GW of data centers by 2030, supporting massive compute demand and sustainable growth.

Is CRWV Stock a Good Buy?

On TipRanks, CoreWeave has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on nine Buys and eight Hold ratings. The average CoreWeave price target of $118.57 implies 30.3% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, CRWV shares have skyrocketed over 145%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth
